TEXT.source: NTU Corpus stories for Seediq, story: sdqNr-sisin_dakis 2020s
TEXT.citation: Sung, L. M., Lily, I., Hsieh, F., & Lin, Z. (2008). Developing an online corpus of Formosan languages. Taiwan Journal of Linguistics, 6(2.).
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @article{sung2008developing,title={Developing an online corpus of Formosan languages.},author={Sung, Li-May and Lily, I and Hsieh, Fuhui and Lin, Zhemin and others},journal={Taiwan Journal of Linguistics},volume={6},number={2},year={2008}}

S.FORM: m mprengoku manu di.
S.PHON: m mpɾeŋoku manu di.
S.FORM: m mprengoku manu di.
S.PHON: m mpɾeŋoku manu di.
S.TRANSL: 嗯..我要說什麼呢?
Well, what should I say?

S.FORM: kari pnslt pnslutucanna ima di rudan na alangmiyan gluban.
S.PHON: kaɾi pnslt pnslutuʦanna ima di ɾudan na alaŋmijan gluban.
S.FORM: kari pnslt pnslutucanna ima di rudan na alangmiyan gluban.
S.PHON: kaɾi pnslt pnslutuʦanna ima di ɾudan na alaŋmijan gluban.
S.TRANSL: 有個我們清流部落的族老流傳下來的故事.
There is a story told by the elderly people of our Gluban tribe.

S.FORM: ma kari nii we bnhanganmu yaani wada maxan hari knkawas cbeyo di ha.
S.PHON: ma kaɾi nii we bnħaŋanmu jaani wada maxan ħaɾi knkawas ʦbejo di ħa.
S.FORM: ma kari nii we bnhanganmu yaani wada maxan hari knkawas cbeyo di ha.
S.PHON: ma kaɾi nii we bnħaŋanmu jaani wada maxan ħaɾi knkawas ʦbejo di ħa.
S.TRANSL: 我很久以前聽到這則故事.大約快十年了.
I heard about this story long time ago. It has been almost ten years.

S.FORM: a uka ka rudan nii uri di.
S.PHON: a uka ka ɾudan nii uɾi di.
S.FORM: a uka ka rudan nii uri di.
S.PHON: a uka ka ɾudan nii uɾi di.
S.TRANSL: 這位族老已不在了.
This elderly has passed away.

S.FORM: e ba ciida na dah daha ka rudan tngesa yaku kari nii kingan we skpawan a sk a tiwas pawan kingan we sk a a pawan ma mesa ka rudan tsk cbeyo si we ita seediq si peni na meniqta alang kesaun truwan peni e bale naq bale cbeyo uri.
S.PHON: e ba ʦiida na daħ daħa ka ɾudan tŋesa jaku kaɾi nii kiŋan we skpawan a sk a tiwas pawan kiŋan we sk a a pawan ma mesa ka ɾudan tsk ʦbejo si we ita seediq si peni na meniqta alaŋ kesaun tɾuwan peni e bale naq bale ʦbejo uɾi.
S.FORM: e ba ciida na dah daha ka rudan tngesa yaku kari nii kingan we skpawan a sk a tiwas pawan kingan we sk a a pawan ma mesa ka rudan tsk cbeyo si we ita seediq si peni na meniqta alang kesaun truwan peni e bale naq bale cbeyo uri.
S.PHON: e ba ʦiida na daħ daħa ka ɾudan tŋesa jaku kaɾi nii kiŋan we skpawan a sk a tiwas pawan kiŋan we sk a a pawan ma mesa ka ɾudan tsk ʦbejo si we ita seediq si peni na meniqta alaŋ kesaun tɾuwan peni e bale naq bale ʦbejo uɾi.
S.TRANSL: 然後,族老說,聽說我們賽德克人以前住在所謂的Truwan地區.以前也真的是如此.
And the elderlies said it is said that we Seediq people used to live in the so-called Truwan area. It was really so in the past.

S.FORM: a klaali na ini kelai mesa a inita kelai ka karac han na.
S.PHON: a klaali na ini kelai mesa a inita kelai ka kaɾaʦ ħan na.
S.FORM: a klaali na ini kelai mesa a inita kelai ka karac han na.
S.PHON: a klaali na ini kelai mesa a inita kelai ka kaɾaʦ ħan na.
S.TRANSL: 平時,我們不能預先知道天氣.
Usually, we do not know the weather beforehand.

S.FORM: mcuwin qmuyux ma mcuwin so m a malu karac peni.
S.PHON: mʦuwin qmujux ma mʦuwin so m a malu kaɾaʦ peni.
S.FORM: mcuwin qmuyux ma mcuwin so m a malu karac peni.
S.PHON: mʦuwin qmujux ma mʦuwin so m a malu kaɾaʦ peni.
S.TRANSL: 有時下雨有時好天氣.
Sometimes it rains, and sometimes it is fine.

S.FORM: ma ita cbeyo na uka ani mmaanu so saya ka bbriganta meniq alang a a a a seediqta cbeyo.
S.PHON: ma ita ʦbejo na uka ani mmaanu so saja ka bbɾiganta meniq alaŋ a a a a seediqta ʦbejo.
S.FORM: ma ita cbeyo na uka ani mmaanu so saya ka bbriganta meniq alang a a a a seediqta cbeyo.
S.PHON: ma ita ʦbejo na uka ani mmaanu so saja ka bbɾiganta meniq alaŋ a a a a seediqta ʦbejo.
S.TRANSL: 而且,以前我們賽德克部落裡,什麼現在的商品都沒有.
And, in the past, we did not have anything like today's goods in our Seediq tribe.

S.FORM: ini so saya de kndalaxta alang de musata hori de musata mari ani mmaanu peni.
S.PHON: ini so saja de kndalaxta alaŋ de musata ħoɾi de musata maɾi ani mmaanu peni.
S.FORM: ini so saya de kndalaxta alang de musata hori de musata mari ani mmaanu peni.
S.PHON: ini so saja de kndalaxta alaŋ de musata ħoɾi de musata maɾi ani mmaanu peni.
S.TRANSL: 那不像現在.現在我們從部落到埔里買各種東西.
It was unlike now. Now we go from our tribe to Hori to buy any kind of things.

S.FORM: kika asi naq mhuma naq so ppuqun kana so a a sama ma so kesaunna ma a bunga peni.
S.PHON: kika asi naq mħuma naq so ppuqun kana so a a sama ma so kesaunna ma a buŋa peni.
S.FORM: kika asi naq mhuma naq so ppuqun kana so a a sama ma so kesaunna ma a bunga peni.
S.PHON: kika asi naq mħuma naq so ppuqun kana so a a sama ma so kesaunna ma a buŋa peni.
S.TRANSL: 所以,我們乾脆食物自己種植,例如蔬菜、地瓜等.
Thus we simply grew food by ourselves, for example, vegetables, and sweet potatoes.

S.FORM: kika eniqan a a kingan na manu di stexan sa a knkawas ciida peni ye ye hmnuwa naq uri a mndndengu ba karac paru.
S.PHON: kika eniqan a a kiŋan na manu di stexan sa a knkawas ʦiida peni je je ħmnuwa naq uɾi a mndndeŋu ba kaɾaʦ paɾu.
S.FORM: kika eniqan a a kingan na manu di stexan sa a knkawas ciida peni ye ye hmnuwa naq uri a mndndengu ba karac paru.
S.PHON: kika eniqan a a kiŋan na manu di stexan sa a knkawas ʦiida peni je je ħmnuwa naq uɾi a mndndeŋu ba kaɾaʦ paɾu.
S.TRANSL: 然後,一…聽說有一次,也不知怎麼搞的,那一年有一場嚴重的旱災.
Then, one... it is said that once, no one knows why, there was a really severe drought that year.

S.FORM: ma mndndengu ba karac paru ciida na.
S.PHON: ma mndndeŋu ba kaɾaʦ paɾu ʦiida na.
S.FORM: ma mndndengu ba karac paru ciida na.
S.PHON: ma mndndeŋu ba kaɾaʦ paɾu ʦiida na.
S.TRANSL: 那是非常嚴重的旱災.
It was a really severe drought.

S.FORM: hmuwa ka huwa ka plaale han wa yaani wada kingan idas daha idas han we eniqan ka so ppuqundaha pnneniqandaha repun peni.
S.PHON: ħmuwa ka ħuwa ka plaale ħan wa jaani wada kiŋan idas daħa idas ħan we eniqan ka so ppuqundaħa pnneniqandaħa ɾepun peni.
S.FORM: hmuwa ka huwa ka plaale han wa yaani wada kingan idas daha idas han we eniqan ka so ppuqundaha pnneniqandaha repun peni.
S.PHON: ħmuwa ka ħuwa ka plaale ħan wa jaani wada kiŋan idas daħa idas ħan we eniqan ka so ppuqundaħa pnneniqandaħa ɾepun peni.
S.TRANSL: 起初還不嚴重.約一兩個月過去,他們倉庫裡還有食物.
At first, it was not serious. About one or two months later, there was still food put in their stockhouse.

S.FORM: ma mkteru idas de bale ba da u uka ba irih so uka bale ppuqun ka kana seediq alang di.
S.PHON: ma mkteɾu idas de bale ba da u uka ba iɾiħ so uka bale ppuqun ka kana seediq alaŋ di.
S.FORM: ma mkteru idas de bale ba da u uka ba irih so uka bale ppuqun ka kana seediq alang di.
S.PHON: ma mkteɾu idas de bale ba da u uka ba iɾiħ so uka bale ppuqun ka kana seediq alaŋ di.
S.TRANSL: 然後三個月了.幾乎什麼都沒有.也就是說,整個賽德克部落沒有食物.
Then three months passed. There was almost nothing. In other words, there was no food in the whole Seediq tribe

S.FORM: ma kika ciida bale ba uka ppuqun di na laqi so pga so sgaalu di peni.
S.PHON: ma kika ʦiida bale ba uka ppuqun di na laqi so pga so sgaalu di peni.
S.FORM: ma kika ciida bale ba uka ppuqun di na laqi so pga so sgaalu di peni.
S.PHON: ma kika ʦiida bale ba uka ppuqun di na laqi so pga so sgaalu di peni.
S.TRANSL: 那時候,真的沒有食物,那小孩就可憐了.
At that time, there was really no food, so hildren were pitiful.

S.FORM: deheya ka plaale so mhuqin e kingan ali de wada mgedang kingan ma mcuwin naq we kingan ali de wada mgedang daha.
S.PHON: deħeja ka plaale so mħuqin e kiŋan ali de wada mgedaŋ kiŋan ma mʦuwin naq we kiŋan ali de wada mgedaŋ daħa.
S.FORM: deheya ka plaale so mhuqin e kingan ali de wada mgedang kingan ma mcuwin naq we kingan ali de wada mgedang daha.
S.PHON: deħeja ka plaale so mħuqin e kiŋan ali de wada mgedaŋ kiŋan ma mʦuwin naq we kiŋan ali de wada mgedaŋ daħa.
S.TRANSL: 他們先死亡.每一天一個去世.然後有時候一天兩個去世.
They died first. Each day one passed away. And sometimes two passed away each day.

S.FORM: kiya ma uxe nii di mesa ka rudanta si.
S.PHON: kija ma uxe nii di mesa ka ɾudanta si.
S.FORM: kiya ma uxe nii di mesa ka rudanta si.
S.PHON: kija ma uxe nii di mesa ka ɾudanta si.
S.TRANSL: 我們的祖先想,這樣怎麼得了.
Our ancestors thought it could not be like this.

S.FORM: ma ha wu a hata haeta theruy icin a yani.
S.PHON: ma ħa wu a ħata ħaeta tħeɾuj iʦin a jani.
S.FORM: ma ha wu a hata haeta theruy icin a yani.
S.PHON: ma ħa wu a ħata ħaeta tħeɾuj iʦin a jani.
S.TRANSL: 我們搬到另一個地方吧!
Let's go move to aother place!

S.FORM: so kii naq ka lngelunganna rudan cbeyo na.
S.PHON: so kii naq ka lŋeluŋanna ɾudan ʦbejo na.
S.FORM: so kii naq ka lngelunganna rudan cbeyo na.
S.PHON: so kii naq ka lŋeluŋanna ɾudan ʦbejo na.
S.TRANSL: 這就是祖先的想法.
This is the thought of the ancestor.

S.FORM: ye yena hili mesa kesaun karac we anaq mndndengu karac ka hini we yaani ini kdengu karac ka icin kika hata theruy icin di mesa ka karina.
S.PHON: je jena ħili mesa kesaun kaɾaʦ we anaq mndndeŋu kaɾaʦ ka ħini we jaani ini kdeŋu kaɾaʦ ka iʦin kika ħata tħeɾuj iʦin di mesa ka kaɾina.
S.FORM: ye yena hili mesa kesaun karac we anaq mndndengu karac ka hini we yaani ini kdengu karac ka icin kika hata theruy icin di mesa ka karina.
S.PHON: je jena ħili mesa kesaun kaɾaʦ we anaq mndndeŋu kaɾaʦ ka ħini we jaani ini kdeŋu kaɾaʦ ka iʦin kika ħata tħeɾuj iʦin di mesa ka kaɾina.
S.TRANSL: 他認為即使這裡旱災,另一個地方可能沒有旱災.所以他說「我們遷徙到另一個地方吧」.
They thought even though there is drought here, maybe there is no drought in another place. Thus, he said, "Let's migrate to another place."

S.FORM: pnrn pnrengoandaha de rudan alang ciida.
S.PHON: pnɾn pnɾeŋoandaħa de ɾudan alaŋ ʦiida.
S.FORM: pnrn pnrengoandaha de rudan alang ciida.
S.PHON: pnɾn pnɾeŋoandaħa de ɾudan alaŋ ʦiida.
S.TRANSL: 那時候部落的族老,他們討論.
The elderly in the tribe at that time, they dicussed.

S.FORM: kika inu di wada kika adisundaha kana laqi da ma kana s tnalang hii wa wada mquri wada mquri a saya di na mquri truku mesa ka kari saya da ma cbeyo we mquri daya mesa peni.
S.PHON: kika inu di wada kika adisundaħa kana laqi da ma kana s tnalaŋ ħii wa wada mquɾi wada mquɾi a saja di na mquɾi tɾuku mesa ka kaɾi saja da ma ʦbejo we mquɾi daja mesa peni.
S.FORM: kika inu di wada kika adisundaha kana laqi da ma kana s tnalang hii wa wada mquri wada mquri a saya di na mquri truku mesa ka kari saya da ma cbeyo we mquri daya mesa peni.
S.PHON: kika inu di wada kika adisundaħa kana laqi da ma kana s tnalaŋ ħii wa wada mquɾi wada mquɾi a saja di na mquɾi tɾuku mesa ka kaɾi saja da ma ʦbejo we mquɾi daja mesa peni.
S.TRANSL: 他們帶所有小孩和所有那裡的族人前往現在的Truku.以前是說往上方移動.
They brought all the children and all the tribespeople there and went toward what is now called Truku. In the past, it was called going upward.

S.FORM: ma bale a kari na rudan we qitaunmu lngelungunmu saya we ma rih so maha wada mquri a yayung inu di suku ga lngelungunmu kari na rudan ciida.
S.PHON: ma bale a kaɾi na ɾudan we qitaunmu lŋeluŋunmu saja we ma ɾiħ so maħa wada mquɾi a jajuŋ inu di suku ga lŋeluŋunmu kaɾi na ɾudan ʦiida.
S.FORM: ma bale a kari na rudan we qitaunmu lngelungunmu saya we ma rih so maha wada mquri a yayung inu di suku ga lngelungunmu kari na rudan ciida.
S.PHON: ma bale a kaɾi na ɾudan we qitaunmu lŋeluŋunmu saja we ma ɾiħ so maħa wada mquɾi a jajuŋ inu di suku ga lŋeluŋunmu kaɾi na ɾudan ʦiida.
S.TRANSL: 祖先的意思,現在我看來,是要朝Suku河前進.我想這是祖先當時的意思.
What the ancestor meant, now I think, was to go toward the Suku river. I think this is what ancestors meant.

S.FORM: ma ma kika seediq pneyah truwan di na kana la laqi apaanundaha awakandaha peni ma kika dudulundaha kana rudan kika mquri inu di alang truwan a mqu mquri a inu di alang truku saya ka REDosaundaha.
S.PHON: ma ma kika seediq pnejaħ tɾuwan di na kana la laqi apaanundaħa awakandaħa peni ma kika dudulundaħa kana ɾudan kika mquɾi inu di alaŋ tɾuwan a mqu mquɾi a inu di alaŋ tɾuku saja ka ɾedosaundaħa.
S.FORM: ma ma kika seediq pneyah truwan di na kana la laqi apaanundaha awakandaha peni ma kika dudulundaha kana rudan kika mquri inu di alang truwan a mqu mquri a inu di alang truku saya ka REDosaundaha.
S.PHON: ma ma kika seediq pnejaħ tɾuwan di na kana la laqi apaanundaħa awakandaħa peni ma kika dudulundaħa kana ɾudan kika mquɾi inu di alaŋ tɾuwan a mqu mquɾi a inu di alaŋ tɾuku saja ka ɾedosaundaħa.
S.TRANSL: 所以,這些來自Truwan的族人,他們背著全部的小孩,扶著全部的老人,朝現今的Truku部落移動.
Therefore, these people from Truwan, they carried all the children on their back and held and supported all the elderly, and they went toward today's Truku tribe.

S.FORM: ma lmngelung mahata ba dehuk inu di mesa.
S.PHON: ma lmŋeluŋ maħata ba deħuk inu di mesa.
S.FORM: ma lmngelung mahata ba dehuk inu di mesa.
S.PHON: ma lmŋeluŋ maħata ba deħuk inu di mesa.

TRANSL.notes: 然後,(他們)想,「我們會到哪裡?」

S.TRANSL: 然後, 想,「我們會到哪裡?」

TRANSL.notes: Then, (they) thought, "Where will we arrive?"

S.TRANSL: Then, thought, "Where will we arrive?"

S.FORM: ma wada thuwe a mkkesa deheya ma bale ba uka naq riyung ppuqun ciida na.
S.PHON: ma wada tħuwe a mkkesa deħeja ma bale ba uka naq ɾijuŋ ppuqun ʦiida na.
S.FORM: ma wada thuwe a mkkesa deheya ma bale ba uka naq riyung ppuqun ciida na.
S.PHON: ma wada tħuwe a mkkesa deħeja ma bale ba uka naq ɾijuŋ ppuqun ʦiida na.
S.TRANSL: 他們慢慢地走.當時真的幾乎已經沒有食物.
They walked slowly. At that time, there was barely any food.

S.FORM: irih so ani naq so m hei qhuni mmaanu odaandaha mekan elu uri di.
S.PHON: iɾiħ so ani naq so m ħei qħuni mmaanu odaandaħa mekan elu uɾi di.
S.FORM: irih so ani naq so m hei qhuni mmaanu odaandaha mekan elu uri di.
S.PHON: iɾiħ so ani naq so m ħei qħuni mmaanu odaandaħa mekan elu uɾi di.
S.TRANSL: 路上經過的樹的果子他們都吃.
They ate all the fruits from trees they passed on the road.

S.FORM: ma ciida a ciida na a cbeyo cbeyo cbeyo cbeyo riyung ka kari nii hayan na.
S.PHON: ma ʦiida a ʦiida na a ʦbejo ʦbejo ʦbejo ʦbejo ɾijuŋ ka kaɾi nii ħajan na.
S.FORM: ma ciida a ciida na a cbeyo cbeyo cbeyo cbeyo riyung ka kari nii hayan na.
S.PHON: ma ʦiida a ʦiida na a ʦbejo ʦbejo ʦbejo ʦbejo ɾijuŋ ka kaɾi nii ħajan na.
S.TRANSL: 那時候,這口傳故事是則很久很久以前的故事.
At that time, this is a story of long long time ago.

S.FORM: ciida si na kana seediq daka c cmcamac peni na mgkela kari.
S.PHON: ʦiida si na kana seediq daka ʦ ʦmʦamaʦ peni na mgkela kaɾi.
S.FORM: ciida si na kana seediq daka c cmcamac peni na mgkela kari.
S.PHON: ʦiida si na kana seediq daka ʦ ʦmʦamaʦ peni na mgkela kaɾi.

TRANSL.notes: (傳述者說)在那個年代裡,人類與各種動物知道對方語言.

S.TRANSL: 在那個年代裡,人類與各種動物知道對方語言.
The storyteller said, at that time, people and all kinds of animals could know each other's language.

S.FORM: kika m m mkleeluw mmaha s a skadi neniqun a a k neniqundaha knbra osaundaha k knbgurah meniq we smiling ka so qbheni ma so camac peni.
S.PHON: kika m m mkleeluw mmaħa s a skadi neniqun a a k neniqundaħa knbɾa osaundaħa k knbguɾaħ meniq we smiliŋ ka so qbħeni ma so ʦamaʦ peni.
S.FORM: kika m m mkleeluw mmaha s a skadi neniqun a a k neniqundaha knbra osaundaha k knbgurah meniq we smiling ka so qbheni ma so camac peni.
S.PHON: kika m m mkleeluw mmaħa s a skadi neniqun a a k neniqundaħa knbɾa osaundaħa k knbguɾaħ meniq we smiliŋ ka so qbħeni ma so ʦamaʦ peni.

TRANSL.notes: 所以在他們找要居住之地的路上、去開墾新地時,(他們)向飛禽走獸們請益.

S.TRANSL: 所以在他們找要居住之地的路上、去開墾新地時, 向飛禽走獸們請益.

TRANSL.notes: Thus, on their way to find a new place to live, to go and reclaim a land, (they) consulted with fowls and beasts.

S.TRANSL: Thus, on their way to find a new place to live, to go and reclaim a land, consulted with fowls and beasts.

S.FORM: ganamu hmuwa di mahanamu inu mesa si.
S.PHON: ganamu ħmuwa di maħanamu inu mesa si.
S.FORM: ganamu hmuwa di mahanamu inu mesa si.
S.PHON: ganamu ħmuwa di maħanamu inu mesa si.

TRANSL.notes: 「你們怎麼了?你們要去哪裡?」(牠們)說.

S.TRANSL: 「你們怎麼了?你們要去哪裡?」 說.

TRANSL.notes: "What happened to you? Where are you going?" (they) said.

S.TRANSL: "What happened to you? Where are you going?" said.

S.FORM: ma kika pneeruun ka pneeruundaha rih so mgd rih so m mg maanu di ado ka mggaluk kari ciida ka kesaun cmcamac daka seediq kika mkela mbahang uri ma mkela naq mprengo uri.

TRANSL.notes: 先祖們就告訴了(牠們).因為當時動物和人可以溝通,所以原來就聽得懂也會說語言對方的語言.

TRANSL.notes: Ancestors told (them). Since at that time animals and people could communicate, they could understand and speak each other's language.

S.TRANSL: Ancestors told . Since at that time animals and people could communicate, they could understand and speak each other's language.

When animals heard about that, they said, "How unfortunate you are! Tell us how to handle this."

TRANSL.notes: How far did they walk and where did they arrive? Now, perhaps (they) are arriving at what is called the Pttingan mountain in our Tgdaya language.

S.TRANSL: How far did they walk and where did they arrive? Now, perhaps are arriving at what is called the Pttingan mountain in our Tgdaya language.

Our tribespeople came from Truwan and arrived there. And animals and people all gather there.

"How should we help the human being?" animals discussed this way.

After that, maybe it was thrush who said, "Let's do this way, shall we?"

S.TRANSL: 我們當中有誰能夠去拿在Pttingan山的大石頭,那個有能力從山丘上帶回石頭到我們在的地方的…那個贏的,能夠將石頭搬來這裡的,他的話之後就是你們的族律.

Among us, the one who can bring a big stone on Pttingan mountain, the one who is capable of bringing back the stone from the top of the hill to where we are…the one who wins and is capable of moving the stone back here, his words will be your tribal law afterwards.

It was proposed by some animals. Elderly people said that was thrush. Elderly people who taught me told me this. The elderly people who taught me said so. The late Tiwas and Pawan said to me, "It was thrush who said so."

TRANSL.notes: (繡眼畫眉)說:「你們賽德克人,因為你們沒有族律所以你們被神懲罰,你們的神.」他們說.

S.TRANSL: 說:「你們賽德克人,因為你們沒有族律所以你們被神懲罰,你們的神.」他們說.

TRANSL.notes: (Thrush said,) "You Seediq people, because you do not have tribal laws, you are punished by God, your God." They said.

S.TRANSL: "You Seediq people, because you do not have tribal laws, you are punished by God, your God." They said.

S.TRANSL: Then, there remained three who did not go and try to move .

What's more, crow's foot was hit and he yelled in pain and ran away. He could not make it.

S.TRANSL: 那個結束後,表示剩下紅嘴黑鵯和繡眼畫眉.
That one was finished, which means there remained black bulbul and thrush.

Black bulbul went first. Sisin thought and said, "You first." Black bulbul said "yes" and went.

Then, there remained only sisin who did not go and move.

The late Tiwas said that people at that time were really sad, saying "What can we do with this?"

People thought, "What is such a small bird capable of doing?"

All the animals as well thought probably no one was capable of taking and carrying that stone.

But thrush did not say anything. He went to that so-called Pttingan mountain.

S.FORM: ma kika didilunna inu dungan ini qbsiyaq dilandaha bale btunux kiya.

People saw thrush raise that stone. They were frightened and all of suddenly became speechless.

S.FORM: ma so hari ka ga mita utux di ma so hari manu di e wada mklui paru peni.

It was like they saw the God. It was also like they were frightened greatly.

Until thrush brought the stone, crossed the river, arrived at the other side, and dropped it where the people gathered.

The ground was all shaking, and people were frightened, thinking, "What is that?" Then people came to at that time.

S.TRANSL: 人們說:「這繡眼畫眉怎麼那麼厲害!」他們全都很高興.
People said, "How powerful thrush is!" They are all very happy.

Thus, from then on, our Seediq people take thrush's words as tribal law.

TRANSL.notes: 有些動物沒趕上(目睹他們)第一次去搬那顆大石頭,到了那邊,又問繡眼畫眉,後悔說:「為什麼我們錯過了?為什麼我們錯過了?」

S.TRANSL: 有些動物沒趕上 第一次去搬那顆大石頭,到了那邊,又問繡眼畫眉,後悔說:「為什麼我們錯過了?為什麼我們錯過了?」

TRANSL.notes: Some animals who failed to (witness them) go to move that big stone for the first time arrived there and asked thrush again, regretting, "Why did we miss it? Why did we miss it?"

S.TRANSL: Some animals who failed to go to move that big stone for the first time arrived there and asked thrush again, regretting, "Why did we miss it? Why did we miss it?"

S.FORM: kika wada inu dungan kesaunna sisin te e hmuwa ka musanamu mangan texan dungan adomiyan so kelaun mesa isu isu bale ka mu mu mnadis btunux nii kesaunna ka sisin ma.

S.FORM: kika wada inu dungan kesaunna sisin te e hmuwa ka musanamu mangan texan dungan adomiyan so kelaun mesa isu isu bale ka mu mu mnadis btunux nii kesaunna ka sisin ma.
S.PHON: kika wada inu duŋan kesaunna sisin te e ħmuwa ka musanamu maŋan texan duŋan adomijan so kelaun mesa isu isu bale ka mu mu mnadis btunux nii kesaunna ka sisin ma.
S.TRANSL: 因此他們去向繡眼畫眉說:「你們再去拿回一顆石頭如何?好讓我們知道這顆石頭是你們搬過來的.」他們跟繡眼畫眉這麼說.
Thus they went and said to thrush, "How about you going again to bring back a huge stone, so that we know it was really you who have brought back this stone?" they said so to thrush.

S.FORM: ini kesai mmaanu ka sisin wada dungan.

Thrush said nothing and left again.

S.TRANSL: 繡眼畫眉又帶回一顆約略同大小的石頭.
Again, thrush brought back another stone of roughly the same size.

S.FORM: didilunna pdehukunna inu di sipo hiya palixanna uri di we daha btunux gaga menaq hiya saya di.
S.PHON: didilunna pdeħukunna inu di sipo ħija palixanna uɾi di we daħa btunux gaga menaq ħija saja di.

S.TRANSL: 他抬起石頭,帶回另一邊,再放下以後,現在那裡有兩顆石頭了.

TRANSL.notes: He lifted (the stone), brought it back to the other side, and dropped it down. Then there are now two stones now.

S.TRANSL: He lifted , brought it back to the other side, and dropped it down. Then there are now two stones now.

S.TRANSL: Pawan說,即使到最近有些老人家到那裡放陷阱,他們經過這個石頭的時候說:「應該要告訴小孩這個故事.」他告訴我.
Pawan said that recently some elderly went there to set traps. When thet passed this stone, they said, "It is necessary that chidren be told this story," he told me.

S.FORM: ma bobona mhedu ka dmidin daha btunux nii ka sisin nii de ma mesa ka ima di seediq dungan ma h gamiyan hmuwa ka yami seediq ha mamiyan knteetu so mnarux peni na ani mnarux mmaanu angalunmiyan mesa.
S.PHON: ma bobona mħedu ka dmidin daħa btunux nii ka sisin nii de ma mesa ka ima di seediq duŋan ma ħ gamijan ħmuwa ka jami seediq ħa mamijan knteetu so mnaɾux peni na ani mnaɾux mmaanu aŋalunmijan mesa.
S.FORM: ma bobona mhedu ka dmidin daha btunux nii ka sisin nii de ma mesa ka ima di seediq dungan ma h gamiyan hmuwa ka yami seediq ha mamiyan knteetu so mnarux peni na ani mnarux mmaanu angalunmiyan mesa.
S.PHON: ma bobona mħedu ka dmidin daħa btunux nii ka sisin nii de ma mesa ka ima di seediq duŋan ma ħ gamijan ħmuwa ka jami seediq ħa mamijan knteetu so mnaɾux peni na ani mnaɾux mmaanu aŋalunmijan mesa.
S.TRANSL: 繡眼畫眉搬完這兩座石頭之後,人們又跟他說:「我們賽德克人是怎麼了?為什麼我們一直生病?我們罹患各種疾病.」
After thrush finished moving these two stones, people said to thrush again, "What's wrong with us Seediq people? Why are we constantly sick? We get all kinds of diseases."

S.FORM: ma ye meya ado uxemiyan mekan meluq mesa ka seediq smiling sisin peni.

"Is this because we eat raw food?" people asked thrush.

"Well, that would be good," thrush said and thought.

"What do you say we do this? Can you go find... the supposed source of fire?"

S.TRANSL: 那個年代還沒有火.我們的祖先還不懂得吃熟食.
At that time, there was no fire. Our ancestors did not know to eat cooked food.

S.FORM: o usa skadi a pusu puniq mesa ka sisin a skagulanna ka ima di a tiyaqung daka a tucaq nii.

S.TRANSL: 「去找火源.」繡眼畫眉說,派遣烏鴉和紅嘴黑鵯.
"Go find the source of fire," thrush said and dispatched crow and black bulbul.

S.FORM: ma ma lmngelung pucaq daka tiyaqung nii we kedu bale beyax na sisin nii asi thei.

Crow and Black bulbul thought of such power thrush had, so they obeyed immediately.

S.FORM: adoka km kmmekun dmidin btunux paru heya na ma lmngelung naq sisin daka tiyaqung nii uri o ma nii si ma niqa ma ga dayoan utux ka heya mesa lmngelung ma asi thei ka pucaq daka ima di tiyaqung nii.

S.TRANSL: 因繡眼畫眉能搬移大石頭,紅嘴黑鵯和烏鴉自己想,他是受到神的幫助.因此紅嘴黑鵯和烏鴉立刻答應.
Because thrush can move the big stone, black bulbul and crow thought to themselves, he is helped by God. Thus black bulbul and crow obeyed immediately.

Then they went downstream to find the so-called source of fire.

All the people and all the animals did not know what that was.

TRANSL.notes: 接著這個人拿來了乾葉子,將(兩顆燧石)相互擦擊點火,真的點出了很多.

S.TRANSL: 接著這個人拿來了乾葉子,將 相互擦擊點火,真的點出了很多.

TRANSL.notes: Then, this person took dry leaves, lit the fire (by knocking the two flint stones against each other), and there were really lots of fire sparks.

S.TRANSL: Then, this person took dry leaves, lit the fire , and there were really lots of fire sparks.

S.PHON: o je kela naq so nii kija kija nii na mesa ka ima di seediq ma mqaɾas bale ka seediq kedusu bale knmalu kesaunna ka puʦaq nii peni.

S.TRANSL: 「原來是這樣啊,」人們說.人們很開心.「你真的很好,」人們對紅嘴黑鵯說.
"So it works this way," people said. People were really happy. "You are really kind," they said to black bulbul.

S.FORM: kndalax ciida di na mkela mutung kika qrari ka rudanta mesa kari pnslutucanna rudanta cbeyo.
S.PHON: kndalax ʦiida di na mkela mutuŋ kika qɾaɾi ka ɾudanta mesa kaɾi pnslutuʦanna ɾudanta ʦbejo.

S.TRANSL: 從那時起,我們祖先就會以摩擦燧石取火,祖先流傳下來的故事是這麼說的.
From then on, our ancestors knew to make fire by rubbing white flints, said our legends inherited from ancestors.

S.FORM: ma menaq ka kesaun menaq a pucaq lmiqu nii na saya di na pucaq lmiqu mesa karimiyan laqi da rudan na pucaq mesa.

S.TRANSL: 此外,有關這紅嘴黑鵯,現在年輕人以我們族語稱為Pucaq lmiqu,而長者們稱之為Pucaq.
Besides, with regard to this black bulbul, now younger people call them Pucaq lmiqu in our language, while elderly people call them Pucaq.

S.FORM: malux ka tanah quwaqna daha papakna na.

S.TRANSL: 何以他的嘴和腳是紅色的呢?
Why are his beak and feet red?

S.PHON: wado ado ado ka bsijaqna madis maanu di a ɾtaħuʦ puniq kii na adisanna deħuk seediq ħini peni.

S.TRANSL: 因為帶餘火到這裡有人的地方要很久.
Because taking the ember to where people were here took a long time.

S.FORM: rih so wada wada hu uxe kesaun wada slamaqun so hari wada slamaqun maanu di rtahuc puniq kika maanu di quwaqna daka papakna ma kika mtanah papakna daka quwaqna pucaq lmiqu dehuk saya mesa ka rudan.

S.TRANSL: 也就是說,紅嘴黑鵯似乎是那時候被餘火燒,所以到現在嘴跟腳都是紅的,族老說.
In other words, black bulbul seemed to be burned by the ember, so his mouth and feet are still red now, the elderly said.

S.TRANSL: 那麼,至於烏鴉呢,有一種說法.我剛才告訴你他被石頭壓到,沒辦法搬動石頭.
Then, as for crow, there is one story. I just told you he was hit by the stone and could not move the stone.

S.FORM: kingan karidaha ge.

S.TRANSL: 紅嘴黑鵯和烏鴉去找火源.
Black bulbul and crow went to find the source of fire.

S.TRANSL: 因為紅嘴黑鵯先找到,所以烏鴉想著:「我們怎麼可以又輸給紅嘴黑鵯?」
Because black bulbul found it first, crow thought, "How can we lose to him again?"

S.FORM: kika osaunna gmeeluk inu naq dung knk kmekulunsu gmeeluk maanu ka pucaq a.
S.PHON: kika osaunna gmeeluk inu naq duŋ knk kmekulunsu gmeeluk maanu ka puʦaq a.

S.TRANSL: 所以烏鴉去搶紅嘴黑鵯,但是你怎能搶得過紅嘴黑鵯呢?
Thus he went to rob black bulbul, but how could you possibly rob black bulbul?

S.FORM: ye ye kii naq beyaxna ps maanu di so ps kmetu ma dmoi papakna ukana ngangalan.
S.PHON: je je kii naq bejaxna ps maanu di so ps kmetu ma dmoi papakna ukana ŋaŋalan.

S.TRANSL: 可能紅嘴黑鵯咬得用力也抓得很用力,所以烏鴉沒辦法拿走.
Black bulbul may have made effort to bite and grab, so crow could not take it away.

S.FORM: ma musa hu hmeri heya musa musa lux maanu di pculing hii ka ima di tiyaqung nii di.
S.PHON: ma musa ħu ħmeɾi ħeja musa musa lux maanu di pʦuliŋ ħii ka ima di tijaquŋ nii di.

S.TRANSL: 烏鴉反而自己無故讓火源給燙傷.
Instead, crow went and got scalded for no reason.

S.FORM: kika dehuk dheran tiyaqung nii na ado ka pnculing ka menaq maanu di skiya baro han na ado pnculing papakna peni kika dehuk dheran di na kika so hari sbbobo icin kkesa na ani dehuk saya tiyaqung nii di so kika kari rudanta.
S.PHON: kika deħuk dħeɾan tijaquŋ nii na ado ka pnʦuliŋ ka menaq maanu di skija baɾo ħan na ado pnʦuliŋ papakna peni kika deħuk dħeɾan di na kika so ħaɾi sbbobo iʦin kkesa na ani deħuk saja tijaquŋ nii di so kika kaɾi ɾudanta.
S.FORM: kika dehuk dheran tiyaqung nii na ado ka pnculing ka menaq maanu di skiya baro han na ado pnculing papakna peni kika dehuk dheran di na kika so hari sbbobo icin kkesa na ani dehuk saya tiyaqung nii di so kika kari rudanta.
S.PHON: kika deħuk dħeɾan tijaquŋ nii na ado ka pnʦuliŋ ka menaq maanu di skija baɾo ħan na ado pnʦuliŋ papakna peni kika deħuk dħeɾan di na kika so ħaɾi sbbobo iʦin kkesa na ani deħuk saja tijaquŋ nii di so kika kaɾi ɾudanta.
S.TRANSL: 因此,當烏鴉到達地面時,因為他的腳在上面飛行的時候被燙傷,所以直到今日走路起來還是搖搖晃晃的.我們的先祖是這麼傳述的.
Thus, when crow arrived on the ground, because his feet were scalded when he flew above, he swayed when walking even to this day. Our ancestors told the story like this.

S.FORM: ma ani dehuk saya na me mesa ka rudan kesaun nosaanna skadi maanu di pusu puniq nii na kiya ka alangta tongan saya kesaundaha sa nosaanna skadi qrari pucaq daka ima di tiyaqung nii peni.
S.PHON: ma ani deħuk saja na me mesa ka ɾudan kesaun nosaanna skadi maanu di pusu puniq nii na kija ka alaŋta toŋan saja kesaundaħa sa nosaanna skadi qɾaɾi puʦaq daka ima di tijaquŋ nii peni.
S.FORM: ma ani dehuk saya na me mesa ka rudan kesaun nosaanna skadi maanu di pusu puniq nii na kiya ka alangta tongan saya kesaundaha sa nosaanna skadi qrari pucaq daka ima di tiyaqung nii peni.
S.PHON: ma ani deħuk saja na me mesa ka ɾudan kesaun nosaanna skadi maanu di pusu puniq nii na kija ka alaŋta toŋan saja kesaundaħa sa nosaanna skadi qɾaɾi puʦaq daka ima di tijaquŋ nii peni.
S.TRANSL: 然後,到現在據說他們去找過火源的源頭.那是我們現在的Tongan部落,他們說.紅嘴黑鵯和烏鴉去那裡尋找燧石.
Then, to this day, it is said that the elderly have gone to look for the origin of the source of fire. That is our Tongan tribe now, they said. Black bulbul and crow went there to find a white flint.

S.FORM: ma ki ma kiya ani dehuk saya na kesaun tongan nii na kari kesaun tongan nii na putungan k pusu na kari we putungan.
S.PHON: ma ki ma kija ani deħuk saja na kesaun toŋan nii na kaɾi kesaun toŋan nii na putuŋan k pusu na kaɾi we putuŋan.
S.FORM: ma ki ma kiya ani dehuk saya na kesaun tongan nii na kari kesaun tongan nii na putungan k pusu na kari we putungan.
S.PHON: ma ki ma kija ani deħuk saja na kesaun toŋan nii na kaɾi kesaun toŋan nii na putuŋan k pusu na kaɾi we putuŋan.

TRANSL.notes: 即使到今天,那被稱做Tongan的地方,所說的Tongan這個字,詞源就是p-utung-an(擦擊點火).

S.TRANSL: 即使到今天,那被稱做Tongan的地方,所說的Tongan這個字,詞源就是p-utung-an .
And even to this day, the place called Tongan, the so-called Tongan this word, its word root is p-utung-an.

S.FORM: ma wada menaq wada musa tnalang hiya seediqta de wadadaha wadadaha maanu di priyuxun mesa tongan di.
S.PHON: ma wada menaq wada musa tnalaŋ ħija seediqta de wadadaħa wadadaħa maanu di pɾijuxun mesa toŋan di.

S.PHON: ma wada menaq wada musa tnalaŋ ħija seediqta de wadadaħa wadadaħa maanu di pɾijuxun mesa toŋan di.
S.TRANSL: 人們在那裡建立部落,然後他們就換稱為Tongan.
People built up a tribe there, and they changed the name into Tongan.

S.FORM: zi bale bale na e kesaun pu a pkesaun a tongan nii na putungan bale ba ka ngayanna.

S.TRANSL: 事實上,Tongan這個地方,他的名字原來真的是ptungan.
In fact, Tongan this place, its name was really ptungan.

S.FORM: ma ye bale ba uri ye ye ga inu saya kesaunna qra qrari naq knmalu kiya sn snlayan pucaq kii iniku kela uri.
S.PHON: ma je bale ba uɾi je je ga inu saja kesaunna qɾa qɾaɾi naq knmalu kija sn snlajan puʦaq kii iniku kela uɾi.
S.FORM: ma ye bale ba uri ye ye ga inu saya kesaunna qra qrari naq knmalu kiya sn snlayan pucaq kii iniku kela uri.

S.TRANSL: 這是真的嗎?紅嘴黑鵯找到好的白燧石的地方在哪裡?我也不知道.
Was it true? Where is the real place where black bulbul found the nice white flints? I don't know, either.

S.FORM: kiya naq dehuk hini karimu.
S.PHON: kija naq deħuk ħini kaɾimu.
S.FORM: kiya naq dehuk hini karimu.
